name: dialoguer
what_it_does: Rust library for building interactive command-line user interfaces with prompts, selections, and confirmations. Part of the console-rs ecosystem. Provides Input (text input), Select (single choice), MultiSelect, Confirm (yes/no), and Password (hidden input) prompts with fuzzy search support. Styled output via console crate.
use_cases: Build interactive Rust CLI setup wizards with text inputs, password prompts, and confirmation dialogs
Implement multi-select menus for choosing configuration options in CLI tools
Create deployment confirmation prompts: Confirm::new().with_prompt('Deploy to production?').interact()?
Build CLI tools with fuzzy-searchable selection lists for long option lists
Implement secure password/secret input without echoing with Password prompt
not_for: Full TUI applications with layouts — use Bubble Tea (Go) or ratatui (Rust) for multi-pane terminal UIs
Non-interactive batch automation — dialoguer prompts require user input; not suitable for scripted automation
best_when: Adding interactive prompts to Rust CLI tools for setup, configuration, or deployment confirmation.
avoid_when: Building fully automated tools without user interaction or complex terminal layouts.

performance.rate_limits: None — local library. Human input speed limited.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: interact() returns io::Error on terminal issues. Validation errors from validators show custom messages. EOF on stdin (piped input) returns error.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Stateless prompt rendering — each prompt is independent.
known_agent_gotchas: interact() blocks until user input — tests with dialoguer prompts require mock stdin; use interact_on_opt() for optional prompts with default
interact_opt() returns Option<T> where None means the user pressed Escape — check for None to handle cancel correctly
Select::with_prompt() is required — without a prompt the selection renders without context, confusing users
fuzzy search in Select requires enabling the 'fuzzy' feature flag — not enabled by default
Password prompt input is hidden but not encrypted in memory — clear secrets after use with zeroize crate
dialoguer requires a real TTY — piped stdin will cause interact() to return an error; detect TTY with atty crate and skip prompts in non-interactive mode

security_score_components.security_notes: Password prompts hide input but secrets remain in memory — use zeroize for sensitive input cleanup.
